a:r.a:t: Application Details of DQN Algorithm in Gomoku
Before inputting Gomoku game data into the DQN algorithm, preprocessing is required, converting game situations into numerical data and encoding win - loss information.

State representation—convert game situations into neural - network input; Q - value calculation—compute Q - values for each feasible action via the neural network; loss - function calculation—adjust neural - network weights based on the difference between actual and predicted results.

During training, the greedy strategy selects the action with the maximum Q - value as the current strategy. In actual games, different strategies can be chosen to balance exploration and exploitation.
